evidence: The docstring's statement — linear forms a_i*n+b_i with no fixed prime divisor of the product are simultaneously prime for infinitely many n — is precisely Dickson's conjecture on prime values of linear forms.
verify_note: Decl exists (@[category research open, AMS 11]); statement is exactly Dickson's conjecture (linear forms a_i n+b_i, no fixed prime divisor, infinitely many simultaneous primes; nat-b restriction equivalent by shifting n); QID live, label matches

evidence: The module header titles the problem the 'Prime Tuples Conjecture', asserting infinitude of prime k-tuples of the linear forms a_i*n+b_i.
verify_note: Decl exists; file/header titled 'Prime Tuples Conjecture' and statement is about prime k-tuples of linear forms; QID live, label matches

evidence: Reed's conjecture bounds the chromatic number chi(G) by ceil((omega(G)+Delta(G)+1)/2) per the docstring.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 5); chi(G) is the bounded quantity in Reed's conjecture - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: The docstring defines omega(G) as 'the size of the largest clique subgraph', i.e. the clique number, one of the two bounding quantities in Reed's conjecture.
verify_note: Decl exists; omega(G) (clique number) is one of the two bounding quantities - load-bearing; QID live, label matches

evidence: The finite-graph variant states the same Reed bound chi(G) <= ceil((omega(G)+Delta(G)+1)/2) on the chromatic number.
verify_note: Decl reed_omega_delta_chi_conjecture_for_finite_graphs exists (research open); same Reed bound on the chromatic number; QID live

evidence: The docstring says 'This problem's name comes from the fact that it is a generalization of Prince Rupert's Cube'.
verify_note: Decl exists (research solved, formal_proof attr); docstring line ~60 explicitly cites Prince Rupert's Cube as the origin/special case; QID live

evidence: The question 'are all convex polyhedra with nonempty interior Rupert?' quantifies over convex polyhedra given as convex hulls of finite vertex sets.
verify_note: Decl exists; the question quantifies over convex polyhedra (convex hulls of finite vertex sets) - load-bearing; QID live, label matches

evidence: The docstring states the conjecture strengthens Huang's sensitivity theorem, which 'gives a quartic bound, bs(f) <= s(f)^4, thereby settling the original conjecture', to a quadratic bound.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 68); docstring states the conjecture strengthens Huang's sensitivity theorem quartic bound to quadratic - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: The conjecture is stated 'for every Boolean function f : {0,1}^n -> {0,1}', bounding its block sensitivity by its sensitivity squared.
verify_note: Decl exists; conjecture is stated for every Boolean function f:{0,1}^n->{0,1} - the objects of the statement; QID live

evidence: The classical Voronovskaja asymptotic formula concerns the Bernstein operators built from Bernstein basis polynomials p_{n,j}(x) = C(n,j) x^j (1-x)^(n-j).
verify_note: Decl voronovskaja_theorem.bernstein_operators exists (research solved); classical Voronovskaja formula is about Bernstein operators/polynomials - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: The open problem asks for the Voronovskaja-type asymptotics of the Bezier variant B_{n,alpha} of the Bernstein operators, defined via powers of tails of Bernstein polynomials.
verify_note: Decl voronovskaja_theorem.bezier_bernstein_operators exists (research open); Bezier variant built from tails of Bernstein polynomials - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: The module header points to 'Fuglede's spectral set conjecture, which motivates the study of weak tilings' for these problems from arXiv:2506.23631.
verify_note: Decl exists and QID is live, but Fuglede's conjecture appears only in a 'See also ... which motivates' note in the module header; it is not load-bearing in the informal statement of problem_4_1 (weak tiling measures for unions of intervals) - tangential motivation

evidence: Problem 3 in [Ar2013] asks for a ZFC example of a weakly first countable compact Hausdorff space 'which is not first countable'.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 54); problem asks for a space that is NOT first countable - load-bearing; QID live, label matches

evidence: Problem 2 in [Ar2013] asks for a weakly first-countable compact Hausdorff space X whose cardinality exceeds the continuum (c < |X|).
verify_note: Decl existsWeaklyFirstCountableCompactBig exists (research open); statement asks whether |X| can exceed the continuum (c < |X|) - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: The docstring's blanket convention makes the sought space compact Hausdorff (CompactSpace X and T2Space X in the statement).
verify_note: Decl exists; compactness (CompactSpace X, T2Space X) is an essential hypothesis of the problem, not generic scaffolding; QID live, label matches

evidence: The docstring says 'Under CH, such a space ... exists as constructed in [Ya1976] by Yakovlev', with the hypothesis Fact (aleph-1 = c).
verify_note: Decl WeaklyFirstCountable.CH.existsWeaklyFirstCountableCompactNotFirstCountable exists (research solved); CH is the explicit hypothesis (Fact aleph1=c, Yakovlev construction) - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: Zagier's conjecture [Za94] concerns the Q-dimension of the space spanned by multiple zeta values zeta(s_1,...,s_k) of weight n.
verify_note: Decl zagier_conjecture exists (research open, AMS 11); conjecture is about Q-dimension of spans of multiple zeta values - load-bearing; QID live

evidence: The solved upper bound [Te02, DG05] states the dimension of the Q-vector space of multiple zeta values of weight n is at most d_n.
verify_note: Decl zagier_upper_bound exists (research solved); the bound is on the dimension of the space of multiple zeta values of weight n - load-bearing; QID live
